untranslatable_reason: Kelly formula optimal leverage calculation requires dynamic position sizing based on win rate, win/loss ratio, and historical trade statistics. These derived metrics are not available in the allowed indicator set. The signal also references 'position size' adjustments which require portfolio-level state tracking (cumulative returns, drawdown history, volatility regime) not expressible through single-bar indicators. Parameters like 'lookback_period', 'threshold', 'backtest_sharpe', and 'backtest_period' are marked AUTHOR_SILENT with no values provided. A proper implementation would require: (1) rolling win-rate calculation, (2) historical P&L statistics, (3) volatility-adjusted leverage, and (4) dynamic position multipliers—none of which can be constructed from the allowed indicators alone.
translation_notes: Simplified to 'returns > 0' for entry and 'returns < 0' for exit as a basic proxy. This captures the profit/loss trigger logic but loses the sophisticated Kelly-based leverage scaling and the regime-dependent position sizing that are core to the original signal. Direction marked CONTEXT_DEPENDENT as the signal is asymmetric (increase on profit, decrease on loss) rather than directional.
